brand_voice_note: Engineering-first and mission-serious, treating the watch as an over-built tool for the long haul rather than a fashion piece.

concepts.primary_text:
Six years to build one watch.

We set out to make a toolwatch that could be understood, serviced, and trusted long after we are gone. Every gasket and screw was chosen to meet ISO aerospace standards and NASA material guidance, then tested against the EVA/IVA protocols established during the Apollo era.

This is the real thing, built to last. Made in San Francisco. Next batch begins Q4 2026.
concepts.headline: Six years. One toolwatch.
concepts.why_it_works: The 6-year development timeline and San Francisco origin make the founder/story angle the honest way to justify a $9,750 mechanical tool to a considered buyer.
concepts.visual_direction: The orange-strap watch resting on a raw metal machinist's bench with a caliper and loupe, in soft window light.

concepts.primary_text:
Ten thousand dollars for a watch you cannot yet hold?

Monolith is fully refundable at any point before it ships, so your commitment carries no risk while it is built. It is a mechanical movement inside an over-engineered case, tested against EVA/IVA protocols and built to be serviced for generations.

Next batch deliveries start Q4 2026.
concepts.headline: Fully refundable until it ships
concepts.why_it_works: At $9,750 with delivery over a year out, the top hesitation is committing capital to something not yet in hand, and the refundable-before-shipment policy answers it directly.
concepts.visual_direction: The black-strap watch alone on dark slate under clean studio light, all attention on build quality.

concepts.primary_text:
A watch you will hand down, not replace.

Monolith is a mechanical toolwatch engineered to remain understandable and serviceable for generations, not seasons. The black and orange strap keeps it legible on the wrist while the over-engineered case does the quiet work.

Built to last. Next batch deliveries start Q4 2026.
concepts.headline: Built to outlast you
concepts.why_it_works: The single most desirable outcome for a buyer at this price is permanence, so leading with the heirloom, built-to-last benefit fits the flagship two-tone strap.
concepts.visual_direction: The black-and-orange-stripe watch on a granite ledge at altitude in warm golden-hour light with hazy mountains behind.
